# Feel

> AI-adoptable backend programming language. First-class REST, AI, DB, and auth primitives. Compiles to standalone Go binaries.

Feel is a new programming language designed for the AI-collaboration era. It makes backend APIs writable in fewer lines with a single canonical syntax that AI tools (Claude, Copilot, Cursor) can generate with higher first-try accuracy.

## Key Facts
- Version: v0.18 (experimental)
- Paradigm: functional-imperative, expression-oriented
- Target: backend REST APIs, AI-augmented services, internal tools
- Runtime: Python 3.7+ interpreter (dev) or compiled Go binary (prod)
- DB support: SQLite, MySQL, PostgreSQL
- AI support: Claude (Anthropic), mock provider

## Core Keywords
route, respond, serve, when, otherwise, define, let, do, record, tool, agent, import

## Stdlib Namespaces
db, ai, crypto, auth, session, validate, migrate, cache, mail, queue, security, string, list, map, json, time, file, math

## What Makes Feel Different
1. Single canonical syntax — one way per concept, no style debates
2. Parser-enforced naming conventions (snake_case vars, PascalCase records)
3. Machine-parseable errors with stable codes (E_UNDEFINED_NAME, E_TYPE, etc.)
4. REST API as a keyword (route/respond/serve)
5. AI primitives in stdlib (ai.ask, ai.summarize, ai.classify, ai.chat)
6. Compiles to standalone Go binary — no runtime dependency at deployment

## Repository Structure
- main.py — CLI entry point
- lexer.py, parser.py, interpreter.py — core language
- compile_go.py — Feel → Go transpiler
- stdlib/ — 18 built-in namespaces
- tests/ — test suite
- examples/ — 15+ demo programs

## Author
AgilS121 — language design, direction, implementation partner with Claude (Anthropic)
